case_title: PUBLIC EMPLOYMENT RELATIONS BOARD VS. CALIFORNIA NURSES ASSOCIATION et al

docket_entries.description: LAW AND MOTION 301, (CONTINUED FROM 11 AM TO 3:00 PM AND PUT ON THE RECORD BY THE COURT )THE MATTER WAS ARGUED; EX-PARTE APPLICATION FOR 1) TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER - 2) ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION - TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER IS GRANTED BY THE COURT. SCHEDULE TO BE WORKED OUT AMONG THE PARTIES. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
REQUEST TO CLERK OF DEPT. 301 TO ORDER DELIVERY OF FILE OF WHICH THE COURT WILL BE ASKED TO TAKE JUDICIAL NOTICE FILED BY DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA NURSES ASSOCIATION
INDEX OF NON-CALIFORNIA AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF OSC RE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION AND APPLICATION FOR LEAVE TO INTERVENE FILED BY REAL PARTY IN INTEREST REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA
REPLY TO DEFT'S OPPOS. TO REQ. FOR INJUNCTIVE RELIEF FILED BY PLAINTIFF PUBLIC EMPLOYMENT RELATIONS BOARD
OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE SUBMITTED BY UC IN SUPPORT OF THE REQUEST FOR INJUNCTIVE RELIEF FILED BY DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA NURSES ASSOCIATION

LAW AND MOTION 301, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE REGARDING PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION - PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION WAS GRANTED BY THE COURT. THE COURT FINDS THAT EVEN IF THE COURT WERE TO ASSUME THAT THE REOPENER NEGOTIATIONS HAD NOT ENDED AND THE NO-STRIKE CLAUSE REMAINED SUSPENDED, PERB HAS REASONABLE CAUSE TO BELIEVE THAT THE STRIKE IS NONETHELESS A PRE-IMPASSE UNFAIR LABOR PRACTICE. THE INJUNCTION WILL REMAIN IN EFFECT WITH RESPECT TO THE POSITION TAKEN IN BY THE UNIVERSITY IN ITS REPLY BRIEF. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S APPLICATION FOR LEAVE TO INTERVENE IS GRANTED. PER DIEM FEES ASSESSED BY THE COURT. PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER AS TO FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189 301/PJB

Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Jun-18-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. HARRY J. GIBBONS, SENIOR REGIONAL ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. (916) 322-3198 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
ATTY. ROB HULTENG OF THE LITTLER MENDELSON, P.C., TEL. NO. 433-1940 APPEARING FOR THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, REAL PARTY IN INTEREST;
ATTY. JAMES E. EGGLESTON, ATTY. PAMELA ALLEN & ATTY. M. JANE LAWHON OF THE CALIFORNIA NURSES ASSOCIATION, TEL. NO. (510) 273-2271 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENTS THE CA NURSES ASSOCIATION.

RULING - A R G U E D; Order To Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction - PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION WAS GRANTED BY THE COURT. THE COURT FINDS THAT EVEN IF THE COURT WERE TO ASSUME THAT THE REOPENER NEGOTIATIONS HAD NOT ENDED AND THE NO-STRIKE CLAUSE REMAINED SUSPENDED, PERB HAS REASONABLE CAUSE TO BELIEVE THAT THE STRIKE IS NONETHELESS A PRE-IMPASSE UNFAIR LABOR PRACTICE. THE INJUNCTION WILL REMAIN IN EFFECT WITH RESPECT TO THE POSITION TAKEN IN BY THE UNIVERSITY IN ITS REPLY BRIEF. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S APPLICATION FOR LEAVE TO INTERVENE IS GRANTED. PER DIEM FEES ASSESSED BY THE COURT. PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER AS TO FORM OF ORDER. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189 =301/PJB

ATTY. ALLISON WOODALL, TEL. NO. (510) 987-0933 APPEARING FOR THE INTERVENOR

RULING - (CONTINUED FROM 11 PM. TO 3:00 P.M AND PUT ON THE RECORD BY THE COURT )THE MATTER WAS ARGUED; Ex-Parte Application For 1) Temporary Restraining Order -
2) Order to Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction - TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER IS GRANTED BY THE COURT. SCHEDULE TO BE WORKED OUT AMONG THE PARTIES. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR # 5797
